description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and visible chisel marks. The block is predominantly brownish-gray, with some areas showing more pronounced textural relief, especially on one face that features what appear to be carved or inscribed patterns. The other sides are less detailed, with one face appearing smoother and another showing irregular, natural stone textures. The top surface is relatively flat but not perfectly even, suggesting it could support weight. The block's size and mass indicate it is likely used as a barrier or boundary element in an urban or landscaped environment. Its robust construction and lack of visible joints suggest it is a single, solid piece of stone.
description: A weathered, brownish-gray stone block with rough, uneven surfaces and one face featuring carved or inscribed patterns. The block is solid, heavy, and suitable as a barrier or boundary element.
description_view_0: This view shows a face with some shallow carvings or inscriptions and a rough, blocky texture.
description_view_1: This side is mostly smooth with minimal surface detail, highlighting the natural stone texture.
description_view_2: This face is the least detailed, with a plain, slightly uneven stone surface and no visible carvings.
description_view_3: This view reveals the most intricate carvings or patterns, suggesting it is the front or most decorative side.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or plaza with decorative stonework
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted earth or sand
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with softened edges and some pitting, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible carvings or inscriptions on one face, possibly decorative or symbolic.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: likely interlocked or aligned with other similar blocks in a row or boundary
